title: (No AI) Meta is laying off hundreds of employees as it pours money into AI
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Vector illustration of the Meta logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25546023/STK043_META_CVIRGINIA_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Meta is laying off hundreds of employees across its company, according to reports from [_The New York Times_](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/25/technology/meta-lays-off-700-employees-while-rewarding-top-executives.html), [NBC News](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/social-media/meta-layoffs-hundreds-employees-five-divisions-vr-ai-who-rcna265127), and [_The Information_](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/meta-platforms-lay-hundreds). The job cuts impact workers on Meta's recruiting, social media, and sales teams, along with Reality Labs, the division that develops the company's smart glasses and virtual reality headsets.

"Teams across Meta regularly restructure or implement changes to ensure they're in the best position to achieve their goals," Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton says in an emailed statement to _The Verge_. "Where possible, we are finding other opportunities for employees whose positions may be impacted."

Clayton declined …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/900946/meta-layoffs-hundreds-employees)
contentSnippet:
Meta is laying off hundreds of employees across its company, according to reports from The New York Times, NBC News, and The Information. The job cuts impact workers on Meta's recruiting, social media, and sales teams, along with Reality Labs, the division that develops the company's smart glasses and virtual reality headsets.
"Teams across Meta regularly restructure or implement changes to ensure they're in the best position to achieve their goals," Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton says in an emailed statement to The Verge. "Where possible, we are finding other opportunities for employees whose positions may be impacted."
Clayton declined  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Disney’s big bets on the metaverse and AI slop aren’t going so well
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![Darth Vader in his classic black suit with cape and helmet. Vader is holding his right lightsaber and slashing it towards the viewer.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/05/darth-vader-fortnite-1920x1080-95ea0f1d91f3.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Less than a week into his tenure as [Disney's newly-appointed CEO](https://www.theverge.com/news/872972/disney-ceo-bob-iger-replacement-josh-damaro), Josh D'Amaro is already dealing with two separate crises that have cast a shadow over the company's future plans. [OpenAI is shutting down its Sora image-generation program](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt) just months after [Disney announced](https://www.theverge.com/news/842348/disney-openai-sora-chatgpt-images) a $1 billion dollar collaboration to [bake the tech into Disney Plus](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/842992/disney-openai-sora-ai-slop-partnership). And Fortnite maker Epic is [laying off 1,000 employees](https://www.theverge.com/games/899529/epic-games-layoffs-fortnite) at a time when we've heard basically nothing about [the game studio's $1.5 billion investment deal](https://www.theverge.com/2024/2/7/24065281/disney-epic-games-investment-fortnite-metaverse) with Disney to [build a metaverse](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/4/24261555/epic-disney-fortnite-unreal-fest).

Disney could still integrate generative AI into its streaming service, and we might end up seeing some version of the co …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/streaming/900837/disney-open-ai-sora-epic-fortnite-metaverse)
contentSnippet:
Less than a week into his tenure as Disney's newly-appointed CEO, Josh D'Amaro is already dealing with two separate crises that have cast a shadow over the company's future plans. OpenAI is shutting down its Sora image-generation program just months after Disney announced a $1 billion dollar collaboration to bake the tech into Disney Plus. And Fortnite maker Epic is laying off 1,000 employees at a time when we've heard basically nothing about the game studio's $1.5 billion investment deal with Disney to build a metaverse.
Disney could still integrate generative AI into its streaming service, and we might end up seeing some version of the co …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Inside our approach to the Model Spec
content: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.
contentSnippet: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.

title: (No AI) Introducing the OpenAI Safety Bug Bounty program
content: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.
contentSnippet: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.

title: (No AI) Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more
content: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro, which unlocks longer tracks with structural awareness. We’re also bringing Lyria to more Google products and surfaces.
contentSnippet: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro, which unlocks longer tracks with structural awareness. We’re also bringing Lyria to more Google products and surfaces.

title: (No AI) Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework
content: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.
contentSnippet: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.

creator: Kat Tenbarge
title: (No AI) There’s Something Very Dark About a Lot of Those Viral AI Fruit Videos
dc:creator: Kat Tenbarge
content: With female AI fruit being fart-shamed and even sexually assaulted, there’s a misogynistic undercurrent to the fruit slop microdramas, even as they appear to be cultivating genuine fans.
contentSnippet: With female AI fruit being fart-shamed and even sexually assaulted, there’s a misogynistic undercurrent to the fruit slop microdramas, even as they appear to be cultivating genuine fans.

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) OpenClaw Agents Can Be Guilt-Tripped Into Self-Sabotage
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: In a controlled experiment, OpenClaw agents proved prone to panic and vulnerable to manipulation. They even disabled their own functionality when gaslit by humans.
contentSnippet: In a controlled experiment, OpenClaw agents proved prone to panic and vulnerable to manipulation. They even disabled their own functionality when gaslit by humans.
